global.title: Spain completes testing of Europe’s largest research vanadium BESS
global.summary: Spain's Fundación Ciudad de la Energía (Ciuden), a government energy research foundation, has completed operational testing of a 1 MW/8 MWh vanadium redox flow battery (VRFB) system at its Cubillos del Sil technology center, which it says is the largest vanadium flow battery in Europe dedicated to a

global.title: New solar window concept integrates polymer-dispersed liquid crystal to enable switchable transparency
global.summary: Researchers in the United Kingdom have developed a smart window that combines switchable polymer-dispersed liquid crystal with integrated PV cells, offering controllable transparency and electricity generation. The system balances light, heat, and solar protection while maintaining high visual quali

global.title: Stricter rules for energy retailers as UK smart meter deployment slows
global.summary: UK government plans new obligations for energy suppliers as smart meter deployment slows in Great Britain. Annual additions to smart electricity meter total have fallen each year since 2021. Binding annual milestones expected to come into force from 2027.

global.title: European residential heat pump sales increased in 2025
global.summary: Analysis across 16 European countries finds an around 240,000 increase in the number of residential heat pump sales year-on-year. The European Heat Pump Association attributes the upward trend to governments stabilizing subsidy schemes.

global.title: Ghana plans to procure 200 MW of battery storage
global.summary: Ghana’s Minister for Energy and Green Transition says the country will advertise competitive bidding processes to deploy 200 MW of battery storage across the country. Ghana’s current installed battery capacity stands at 10 MWh.

global.title: Brazil’s LRCAP battery auction demand estimated at nearly 3 GW
global.summary: Brazil's second Long-term Reserve Capacity Auction (LRCAP) of 2026 has contracted 501 MW of thermoelectric capacity, leaving an estimated 3 GW of remaining demand to be addressed in a forthcoming battery storage auction expected later this year.

global.title: German startup offers team of 22 specialized AI workers for PV plant O&M operations
global.summary: Invertix has launched 22 specialized AI “workers” to automate O&M tasks for renewable energy assets, targeting inefficiencies in data handling, alarm management, and reporting. The startup says its system enables PV plant operators to scale portfolios more efficiently by reducing manual workload

global.title: Türkiye’s solar capacity surpasses 25 GW
global.summary: Alparslan Bayraktar, Türkiye's Minister of Energy and Natural Resources, says 2026 will be a record year for renewables in the country after total deployed solar reached 25,827 MW by the end of January.

global.title: When tiny magnets attack PV systems
global.summary: In an interview with pv magazine, cybersecurity expert Mohammad Al Faruque explains how seemingly simple sensors in PV systems and other energy systems are surprisingly vulnerable to magnetic, electrical, and acoustic perturbations, which can remotely influence control systems without physical acces

global.title: Indian industry body projects 346 GWh of BESS capacity by 2033
global.summary: India's cumulative installed battery energy storage system (BESS) capacity is projected to reach 346 GWh by 2033 from less than 1 GWh today, according to a white paper by the Indian Energy Storage Alliance (IESA) and Customized Energy Solutions (CES).

global.title: Truck Lobbying Group Seeks to Join Lawsuit In Support of Trump EPA’s Rollback of Vehicle Standards & Repeal of Endangerment Finding
global.summary: WASHINGTON, D.C. — On Friday, the Truck and Engine Manufacturers Association (EMA) filed a motion to intervene in the lawsuit that the Sierra Club and its partners brought against Trump’s EPA over its repeal of the endangerment finding and vehicle emission standards. In response to EMA’s interventio

global.title: Iran Crisis Shows Why Automakers Are Wrong To Cancel EV Plans
global.summary: Interest in electric cars is way up following increases in gas prices following the unprovoked attack on Iran. The post Iran Crisis Shows Why Automakers Are Wrong To Cancel EV Plans appeared first on CleanTechnica.

global.title: Manila Welcomes Locally Engineered Electric “Tram” to the Walled City
global.summary: The office of The Manila Times, where my “day job” is located, is within the historic walled city of Intramuros where the original tranvias (streetcars) operated during the late Spanish era (starting in 1886). They eventually evolved with the American electric tram system, but that was d

global.title: TotalEnergies Milks Trump For $1 Billion Offshore Wind Payout, Gets Last Laugh
global.summary: The French firm TotalEnergies agrees not to build offshore wind farms in the US -- at least, not for the present time... The post TotalEnergies Milks Trump For $1 Billion Offshore Wind Payout, Gets Last Laugh appeared first on CleanTechnica.

global.summary: The final piece of Hawaiʻi’s decarbonization puzzle is not on Oʻahu’s domestic grid. That part of the work is already largely bounded.
